basics.name: Daniel Ilies
basics.label: Software Engineer | Java, Go, Node.js, Backend Systems
basics.summary: Full-Stack Engineer with 4+ years of production experience in Java, Go, and Node.js backends; delivered measurable improvements in system performance and maintainability; experienced with Docker, AWS, CI/CD, and distributed systems; seeking a software engineering role in a high-scale fintech environment.

work.name: GreenSoft
work.summary: Backend Java development on an EU government project within a professional software team.
highlights: Developed and maintained Java-based backend services for an EU government project, using Form.io for dynamic form management.
Implemented CAPTCHA and email verification logic conditionally based on user authentication state.
Proposed and built a reusable, config-driven form component adopted as the team standard.
Participated in full SDLC including code reviews, testing, and Agile ceremonies.

work.name: DevelBox
work.summary: Led frontend initiatives and engineered ML-integrated solutions in production.
highlights: Led frontend refactor (Vue 2 to Vuetify), improving page load times by ~25%.
Engineered AdCognition: end-to-end ML ad detection system (Python/TensorFlow training + TypeScript/TensorFlow.js Chrome extension) with >90% detection accuracy.
Improved model accuracy by ~15% through iterative dataset tuning and hyperparameter optimization.
Maintained Linux-based ML training infrastructure with Flask services, Docker, and TensorBoard.

work.name: Trood
work.summary: Delivered production features on the Rizq Launcher MVP in a multi-stack environment.
highlights: Delivered end-to-end production features for the Rizq Launcher MVP using Go monolithic backend and Next.js frontend.
Solved complex authorization issues including reliable 2FA management across independent codebases.
Implemented gRPC-based TTS module and a Telegram Bot-Client across Go, Node.js, and Java.

courses: Bachelor Thesis: Building a Modern Fullstack Web Application

awards.title: 1st Place Innovation Contest
awards.summary: Won for 'Otis' automation tool that streamlined templating and email workflows.

projects.name: AdCognition – End-to-End ML Ad Detection System
projects.description: Production Chrome extension for real-time ad detection with Python/TensorFlow training stack and TFJS deployment.
highlights: Achieved >90% detection accuracy; improved by ~15% via iterative tuning.
Built ML pipeline: Python training → TFJS export → Chrome extension deployment.

projects.name: IT-Hub – MERN Stack Social Media Web Application
projects.description: Full-stack social platform with React, Node/Express, MongoDB.
highlights: Implemented authentication, posts, likes, and collaboration features.
Containerized with Docker for consistent development and deployment.
